title: Fly.io Volume
description: A Fly Volume is a persistent block storage resource that can be attached to a Fly Machine for durable data storage. Volumes are region-specific, encrypted by default, and support automatic daily snapshots with configurable retention periods. Volumes are identified by a unique ID and can be extended in size but not shrunk.

id.description: Unique identifier for this Fly Volume.

name.description: Human-readable name for the volume, used for identification within an app.

state.description: Current operational state of the volume.

size_gb.description: Current size of the volume in gigabytes.

region.description: The region code where this volume is provisioned.

zone.description: The availability zone within the region where this volume resides.

encrypted.description: Whether the volume is encrypted at rest using platform-managed keys. Enabled by default for all volumes.

attached_machine_id.description: The ID of the Fly Machine this volume is currently attached to. Null if unattached.

attached_alloc_id.description: The allocation ID of the Machine this volume is attached to.

filesystem_type.description: The filesystem type provisioned on this volume.

snapshot_retention.description: Number of days automatic snapshots are retained. Defaults to 5. Minimum 1, maximum 60.

block_size.description: Block size of the volume filesystem in bytes.

blocks_free.description: Number of free filesystem blocks available for use.

blocks_avail.description: Number of filesystem blocks available to non-root users.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the volume was created.

VolumeSnapshot.description: A point-in-time snapshot of a Fly Volume, used for backup and disaster recovery.

size.description: Size of the snapshot data in bytes.

digest.description: Content hash of the snapshot for data integrity verification.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the snapshot was created.
